agent2 (Round 1 summarized): Metastatic tumors are commonly cited across medical discussions because they often originate elsewhere before spreading to the mediastinum; thus, they represent a large proportion of observed cases there. Agent2 concluded based on this recurring theme that these were the most typical mediastinal mass seen clinically. \boxed{D}
To summarize:

The analysis focuses heavily on metastatic tumors due to their wide distribution from primary sites outside the chest, making them the most frequently occurring mediastinal tumor according to available data. Thus, option \( \textbf{D} \) correctly identifies metastatic tumors as the predominant form present in the mediastinum. 
\boxed{\textbf{D}}. Answer choice: D
